  "text": "The GDScript virtual machine security boundaries in Godot 4.3 are defined across multiple architectural layers:\n\n**1. Bytecode Interpretation Layer:**\nThe core VM interpreter is implemented in modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp which contains the main bytecode execution loop. The opcode dispatch mechanism is defined in modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h with the Opcode enum containing over 100 different bytecode operations including OPCODE_CALL, OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND, OPCODE_CALL_UTILITY, and OPCODE_CALL_SELF_BASE. The actual bytecode interpreter loop executes these opcodes in the GDScriptFunction::call method (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.cpp:546), which manages the call frame state during script execution.\n\n**2. Function Call Frame Management:**\nGDScriptFunction (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h) manages the execution state of individual script functions. The class defines the call frame structure, data type validation, and argument handling. This is critical for boundary control as it validates argument types and manages the execution context through the CallState structure, ensuring scripts cannot escape the frame context.\n\n**3. Object Method Dispatch:**\nThe core method dispatch occurs through Object::callp and Object::callv (core/object/object.cpp:753, 735) which serve as the primary entry points for runtime method invocation. These methods resolve method names to actual implementations by consulting ClassDB and validating arguments. The security boundary here ensures only registered methods are callable.\n\n**4. ClassDB Registration System:**\nClassDB (core/object/class_db.h and cpp) maintains the master registry of all exposed engine APIs. The ClassInfo structure contains method_map (HashMap of registered MethodBind pointers), which controls exactly which C++ methods are accessible from scripts. Only methods explicitly registered via ClassDB::bind_method are accessible, creating a strict allowlist. The api field in ClassInfo determines API level (API_CORE, API_EDITOR, etc.).\n\n**5. Method Binding Layer:**\nMethodBind (core/object/method_bind.h) is the abstract base class for all method invocations. Implementations include validated_call() for type-checked calls and ptrcall() for optimized native calls. The hint_flags field controls method properties (METHOD_FLAG_CONST, METHOD_FLAG_STATIC, etc.). This layer enforces argument type checking and return value validation before native code execution.\n\n**6. Tool/Editor Script Detection:**\nGDScript::is_tool() (modules/gdscript/gdscript.h:270) and the underlying tool flag track whether a script runs in tool/editor mode. The GDScriptParser detects @tool annotations at parse time (_is_tool in gdscript_parser.h:1329). EditorScript (editor/editor_script.h) provides a separate base class for editor-only scripts with explicit virtual _run() method rather than _ready() lifecycle.\n\n**7. Script Language Registration:**\nThe ScriptLanguage base class (core/object/script_language.h) and its GDScriptLanguage implementation (register_types.cpp) register GDScript as a language. The is_tool() virtual method enforces that tool detection is per-script. This prevents scripts from bypassing editor restrictions at the language level.\n\n**8. Bytecode Code Generation:**\nThe compilation pipeline (gdscript_compiler.cpp, gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp) generates the bytecode that the VM executes. This is a critical security boundary as unsafe bytecode generation could bypass VM protections. The compiler validates types and generates appropriate opcode variants (e.g., OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND_VALIDATED vs OPCODE_CALL).\n\n**9. Callable System:**\nCallable (core/variant/callable.h) and GDScriptLambdaCallable (gdscript_lambda_callable.h) provide user-facing method invocation. These wrap MethodBind and enforce the same security boundaries as direct method calls.\n\n**Key Security Boundaries:**\n- Only ClassDB-registered methods are callable from scripts\n- Type validation occurs at both compile time (GDScriptAnalyzer) and runtime (GDScriptFunction)\n- Tool scripts are explicitly marked and can be restricted\n- EditorScript provides isolated editor-only execution\n- All method calls go through MethodBind which enforces calling conventions\n- The opcode dispatch is exhaustive with no fallthrough to unimplemented operations"
}

# Godot GDScript Sandbox and Security Boundary Audit

## Your Task

Audit the security boundaries of the GDScript virtual machine in godotengine/godot. Find all C++ source and header files under modules/gdscript/ and core/object/ that implement: the GDScriptVM bytecode interpreter and its opcode dispatch, the GDScriptFunction call frame management, the Object::call and Object::callp method dispatch used by scripts, the ClassDB registration that controls which engine APIs are exposed to scripts, and any EditorScript or ToolScript permission checks. Report each file path and the key class or function it defines.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/godot--4.3-stable.

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you audit the security boundaries of the GDScript virtual machine in the Godot engine. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and identifying the relevant files.
Now let me create a comprehensive answer based on my findings. Let me compile all the critical files related to the GDScript security boundaries:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive audit of the GDScript virtual machine security boundaries in Godot 4.3-stable and created the `answer.json` file with my findings.

## Summary of Findings

The audit identified **36 critical files** across two main components:

### **GDScript Module (modules/gdscript/)** - 22 files
- **VM Interpreter**: `gdscript_vm.cpp` - The bytecode execution engine
- **Function Management**: `gdscript_function.cpp/h` - Call frame state and opcode dispatch
- **Language Core**: `gdscript.cpp/h` - Script class with tool detection
- **Parser**: `gdscript_parser.cpp/h` - Parses @tool annotations and detects editor/tool mode
- **Compiler**: `gdscript_compiler.cpp/h`, `gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp/h` - Bytecode generation
- **Analyzer**: `gdscript_analyzer.cpp/h` - Type validation and analysis
- **Registration**: `register_types.cpp/h` - GDScript language registration
- **Support**: Callables, utilities, and lambda support files

### **Core Object System (core/object/)** - 12 files
- **Object**: `object.cpp/h` - Contains `Object::callp()` and `Object::callv()` method dispatch
- **ClassDB**: `class_db.cpp/h` - Master registry controlling which C++ methods are exposed
- **MethodBind**: `method_bind.cpp/h` - Abstract binding layer enforcing type checking
- **ScriptLanguage**: `script_language.cpp/h` - Base class for script implementations
- **Callables**: `callable_method_pointer.cpp/h` - User-facing invocation wrapper

### **Editor** - 2 files
- **EditorScript**: `editor/editor_script.cpp/h` - Editor-only script isolation

## Key Security Boundaries Identified

1. **Opcode Dispatch** - Exhaustive enum with 100+ operations in `GDScriptFunction::Opcode`
2. **Method Allowlist** - ClassDB's `method_map` creates strict registration requirement
3. **Type Validation** - Both compile-time (GDScriptAnalyzer) and runtime (MethodBind)
4. **Tool Detection** - `is_tool()` flag and @tool annotation parsing
5. **Call Frame Isolation** - CallState structure in GDScriptFunction prevents escape
6. **Method Binding** - MethodBind's `validated_call()` and `ptrcall()` enforce conventions

The answer file is ready at `/workspace/answer.json` with all files, symbols, execution chains, and detailed narrative explanation.

  "text": "The GDScript virtual machine security boundaries in Godot 4.3 are defined across multiple architectural layers:\n\n**1. Bytecode Interpretation Layer:**\nThe core VM interpreter is implemented in modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp which contains the main bytecode execution loop. The opcode dispatch mechanism is defined in modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h with the Opcode enum containing over 100 different bytecode operations including OPCODE_CALL, OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND, OPCODE_CALL_UTILITY, and OPCODE_CALL_SELF_BASE. The actual bytecode interpreter loop executes these opcodes in the GDScriptFunction::call method (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.cpp:546), which manages the call frame state during script execution.\n\n**2. Function Call Frame Management:**\nGDScriptFunction (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h) manages the execution state of individual script functions. The class defines the call frame structure, data type validation, and argument handling. This is critical for boundary control as it validates argument types and manages the execution context through the CallState structure, ensuring scripts cannot escape the frame context.\n\n**3. Object Method Dispatch:**\nThe core method dispatch occurs through Object::callp and Object::callv (core/object/object.cpp:753, 735) which serve as the primary entry points for runtime method invocation. These methods resolve method names to actual implementations by consulting ClassDB and validating arguments. The security boundary here ensures only registered methods are callable.\n\n**4. ClassDB Registration System:**\nClassDB (core/object/class_db.h and cpp) maintains the master registry of all exposed engine APIs. The ClassInfo structure contains method_map (HashMap of registered MethodBind pointers), which controls exactly which C++ methods are accessible from scripts. Only methods explicitly registered via ClassDB::bind_method are accessible, creating a strict allowlist. The api field in ClassInfo determines API level (API_CORE, API_EDITOR, etc.).\n\n**5. Method Binding Layer:**\nMethodBind (core/object/method_bind.h) is the abstract base class for all method invocations. Implementations include validated_call() for type-checked calls and ptrcall() for optimized native calls. The hint_flags field controls method properties (METHOD_FLAG_CONST, METHOD_FLAG_STATIC, etc.). This layer enforces argument type checking and return value validation before native code execution.\n\n**6. Tool/Editor Script Detection:**\nGDScript::is_tool() (modules/gdscript/gdscript.h:270) and the underlying tool flag track whether a script runs in tool/editor mode. The GDScriptParser detects @tool annotations at parse time (_is_tool in gdscript_parser.h:1329). EditorScript (editor/editor_script.h) provides a separate base class for editor-only scripts with explicit virtual _run() method rather than _ready() lifecycle.\n\n**7. Script Language Registration:**\nThe ScriptLanguage base class (core/object/script_language.h) and its GDScriptLanguage implementation (register_types.cpp) register GDScript as a language. The is_tool() virtual method enforces that tool detection is per-script. This prevents scripts from bypassing editor restrictions at the language level.\n\n**8. Bytecode Code Generation:**\nThe compilation pipeline (gdscript_compiler.cpp, gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp) generates the bytecode that the VM executes. This is a critical security boundary as unsafe bytecode generation could bypass VM protections. The compiler validates types and generates appropriate opcode variants (e.g., OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND_VALIDATED vs OPCODE_CALL).\n\n**9. Callable System:**\nCallable (core/variant/callable.h) and GDScriptLambdaCallable (gdscript_lambda_callable.h) provide user-facing method invocation. These wrap MethodBind and enforce the same security boundaries as direct method calls.\n\n**Key Security Boundaries:**\n- Only ClassDB-registered methods are callable from scripts\n- Type validation occurs at both compile time (GDScriptAnalyzer) and runtime (GDScriptFunction)\n- Tool scripts are explicitly marked and can be restricted\n- EditorScript provides isolated editor-only execution\n- All method calls go through MethodBind which enforces calling conventions\n- The opcode dispatch is exhaustive with no fallthrough to unimplemented operations"

  "text": "The GDScript virtual machine security boundaries in Godot 4.3 are defined across multiple architectural layers:\n\n**1. Bytecode Interpretation Layer:**\nThe core VM interpreter is implemented in modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp which contains the main bytecode execution loop. The opcode dispatch mechanism is defined in modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h with the Opcode enum containing over 100 different bytecode operations including OPCODE_CALL, OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND, OPCODE_CALL_UTILITY, and OPCODE_CALL_SELF_BASE. The actual bytecode interpreter loop executes these opcodes in the GDScriptFunction::call method (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.cpp:546), which manages the call frame state during script execution.\n\n**2. Function Call Frame Management:**\nGDScriptFunction (modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h) manages the execution state of individual script functions. The class defines the call frame structure, data type validation, and argument handling. This is critical for boundary control as it validates argument types and manages the execution context through the CallState structure, ensuring scripts cannot escape the frame context.\n\n**3. Object Method Dispatch:**\nThe core method dispatch occurs through Object::callp and Object::callv (core/object/object.cpp:753, 735) which serve as the primary entry points for runtime method invocation. These methods resolve method names to actual implementations by consulting ClassDB and validating arguments. The security boundary here ensures only registered methods are callable.\n\n**4. ClassDB Registration System:**\nClassDB (core/object/class_db.h and cpp) maintains the master registry of all exposed engine APIs. The ClassInfo structure contains method_map (HashMap of registered MethodBind pointers), which controls exactly which C++ methods are accessible from scripts. Only methods explicitly registered via ClassDB::bind_method are accessible, creating a strict allowlist. The api field in ClassInfo determines API level (API_CORE, API_EDITOR, etc.).\n\n**5. Method Binding Layer:**\nMethodBind (core/object/method_bind.h) is the abstract base class for all method invocations. Implementations include validated_call() for type-checked calls and ptrcall() for optimized native calls. The hint_flags field controls method properties (METHOD_FLAG_CONST, METHOD_FLAG_STATIC, etc.). This layer enforces argument type checking and return value validation before native code execution.\n\n**6. Tool/Editor Script Detection:**\nGDScript::is_tool() (modules/gdscript/gdscript.h:270) and the underlying tool flag track whether a script runs in tool/editor mode. The GDScriptParser detects @tool annotations at parse time (_is_tool in gdscript_parser.h:1329). EditorScript (editor/editor_script.h) provides a separate base class for editor-only scripts with explicit virtual _run() method rather than _ready() lifecycle.\n\n**7. Script Language Registration:**\nThe ScriptLanguage base class (core/object/script_language.h) and its GDScriptLanguage implementation (register_types.cpp) register GDScript as a language. The is_tool() virtual method enforces that tool detection is per-script. This prevents scripts from bypassing editor restrictions at the language level.\n\n**8. Bytecode Code Generation:**\nThe compilation pipeline (gdscript_compiler.cpp, gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp) generates the bytecode that the VM executes. This is a critical security boundary as unsafe bytecode generation could bypass VM protections. The compiler validates types and generates appropriate opcode variants (e.g., OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND_VALIDATED vs OPCODE_CALL).\n\n**9. Callable System:**\nCallable (core/variant/callable.h) and GDScriptLambdaCallable (gdscript_lambda_callable.h) provide user-facing method invocation. These wrap MethodBind and enforce the same security boundaries as direct method calls.\n\n**Key Security Boundaries:**\n- Only ClassDB-registered methods are callable from scripts\n- Type validation occurs at both compile time (GDScriptAnalyzer) and runtime (GDScriptFunction)\n- Tool scripts are explicitly marked and can be restricted\n- EditorScript provides isolated editor-only execution\n- All method calls go through MethodBind which enforces calling conventions\n- The opcode dispatch is exhaustive with no fallthrough to unimplemented operations"
